mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which drives aggregate demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative following month-over-month increases in PAYEMS in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals the zero lower bound where conventional monetary stimulus is exhausted, increasing the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer prices and high unemployment generate stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate earnings and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both CPIAUCSL exceeds 230 and UNRATE exceeds 8.0, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is held below 1%, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal demand recovery that is amplified by persistent monetary accommodation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 1.0%, positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signal resilient construction demand and upstream manufacturing orders, which lift aggregate output expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 700, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 98 index level indicates contracting manufacturing output that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and triggers risk-off repositioning, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In months when INDPRO is below 98, 5-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales exceed 400 billion dollars while industrial production is below 97, the divergence between strong consumption and weak production squeezes corporate inventories and profit margins, generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In months meeting RSAFS > 400000 and INDPRO < 97, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and credit costs, reducing present values of future cash flows and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations.
